rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope when bindings depend on implementation-level details, and into the component type scope when contracts rely on visible ports or properties.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only referencing visible features or explicitly declared constants in AGREE contracts.
Place output range guarantees in component type annexes if based on visible outputs; use implementation annexes only for assignments tied to subcomponent bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Use `eq` to define boolean range predicates from visible inputs/outputs, not AADL properties.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not use subcomponent port paths in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
